data.reasoningText:
This project uses .NET 10 with MSTest 3.8.0 and the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner (as specified in global.json). Let me run the tests with a TRX report.

With `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` runner, the command-line flags are different from the traditional VSTest runner. The TRX report can be generated using `--report-trx` flag.

Let me run `dotnet test --report-trx`.

data.content: The project needs to be configured for the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner. Let me update the `.csproj`:
data.reasoningText:
The project needs to be configured for Microsoft.Testing.Platform. Since the global.json specifies `"runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform"`, but the project isn't set up for it. I need to add the `<UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>true</UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>` property, or use `Microsoft.Testing.Platform.MSBuild` package, or set the `EnableMSTestRunner` property.

With MSTest 3.8.0 and Microsoft.Testing.Platform, I need to:
1. Set `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>` 


2. Set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` 
3. Set `<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>`

These properties will enable the testing platform runner and configure the project as an executable for MSTest 3.x.

data.reasoningText:
The test executable runs fine directly. The issue is with `dotnet test` specifically. In .NET 10, it seems `dotnet test` has a new way of detecting if projects use MTP (Microsoft Testing Platform). Let me look at the documentation.

Let me try with the property `<UseVSTest>false</UseVSTest>` or the `<IsTestingPlatformApplication>true</IsTestingPlatformApplication>` property. 

Actually, let me check the .NET 10 documentation for this. The URL they gave was https://aka.ms/dotnet-test/mtp.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://aka.ms/dotnet-test/mtp:
**This article applies to:** ✔️ .NET 6 SDK and later versions

## Name

`dotnet test` - .NET test driver used to execute unit tests.

## Description

The `dotnet test` command builds the solution and runs the tests with either VSTest or Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP). The test runner you use determines the available command-line options and behavior.

Note

Test runner selection is available starting with .NET 10 SDK. In earlier versions of .NET, tests are always executed with VSTest.

### Choosing a test runner

To enable Microsoft.Testing.Platform, you need to specify the test runner in the [`global.json`](global-json) file:

```
{
    "test": {
        "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform"
    }
}
```

Note

`VSTest` is a valid value for test runner. It is the current default and can be omitted.

Important

The `dotnet test` experience for MTP is only supported in `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` version 1.7 and later.

### Test runner documentation

The available command-line options, behavior, and capabilities differ depending on which test runner you use:

*   **[dotnet test with VSTest](dotnet-test-vstest)** - The traditional test platform, available in .NET 6 SDK and later. This is the default and only test runner in versions earlier than .NET 10 SDK. Provides comprehensive test discovery, filtering, and result reporting capabilities.
    
*   **[dotnet test with MTP](dotnet-test-mtp)** - The modern testing platform, available in .NET 10 SDK and later. Offers faster test execution and more flexible test module selection.
    

## See also

*   [Testing with dotnet test](../testing/unit-testing-with-dotnet-test)
*   [dotnet test with VSTest](dotnet-test-vstest)
*   [dotnet test with MTP](dotnet-test-mtp)
*   [Frameworks and Targets](../../standard/frameworks)
*   [.NET Runtime Identifier (RID) catalog](../rid-catalog)

Contents of https://aka.ms/dotnet-test/mtp:
**This article applies to:** ✔️ .NET 6 SDK and later versions

## Name

`dotnet test` - .NET test driver used to execute unit tests.

## Description

The `dotnet test` command builds the solution and runs the tests with either VSTest or Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP). The test runner you use determines the available command-line options and behavior.

Note

Test runner selection is available starting with .NET 10 SDK. In earlier versions of .NET, tests are always executed with VSTest.

### Choosing a test runner

To enable Microsoft.Testing.Platform, you need to specify the test runner in the [`global.json`](global-json) file:

```
{
    "test": {
        "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform"
    }
}
```

Note

`VSTest` is a valid value for test runner. It is the current default and can be omitted.

Important

The `dotnet test` experience for MTP is only supported in `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` version 1.7 and later.

### Test runner documentation

The available command-line options, behavior, and capabilities differ depending on which test runner you use:

*   **[dotnet test with VSTest](dotnet-test-vstest)** - The traditional test platform, available in .NET 6 SDK and later. This is the default and only test runner in versions earlier than .NET 10 SDK. Provides comprehensive test discovery, filtering, and result reporting capabilities.
    
*   **[dotnet test with MTP](dotnet-test-mtp)** - The modern testing platform, available in .NET 10 SDK and later. Offers faster test execution and more flexible test module selection.
    

## See also

*   [Testing with dotnet test](../testing/unit-testing-with-dotnet-test)

**This article applies to:** ✔️ .NET 6 SDK and later versions

## Name

`dotnet test` - .NET test driver used to execute unit tests.

## Description

The `dotnet test` command builds the solution and runs the tests with either VSTest or Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP). The test runner you use determines the available command-line options and behavior.

Note

Test runner selection is available starting with .NET 10 SDK. In earlier versions of .NET, tests are always executed with VSTest.

### Choosing a test runner

To enable Microsoft.Testing.Platform, you need to specify the test runner in the [`global.json`](global-json) file:

```
{
    "test": {
        "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform"

```

Note

`VSTest` is a valid value for test runner. It is the current default and can be omitted.

Important

The `dotnet test` experience for MTP is only supported in `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` version 1.7 and later.

### Test runner documentation

The available command-line options, behavior, and capabilities differ depending on which test runner you use:

*   **[dotnet test with VSTest](dotnet-test-vstest)** - The traditional test platform, available in .NET 6 SDK and later. This is the default and only test runner in versions earlier than .NET 10 SDK. Provides comprehensive test discovery, filtering, and result reporting capabilities.

data.reasoningText:
So the documentation says that `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` might not be the right property for .NET 10. In .NET 10, the global.json specifies `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` as runner, and the project needs to be set up correctly for it. But `dotnet test` is complaining that the project is using VSTest.

Let me look at what `dotnet test` is checking specifically. It says "The following test projects are using VSTest test runner: TestProject.csproj". 

The issue is that the project needs `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` version 1.7+. Let me check what version of Microsoft.Testing.Platform comes with MSTest 3.8.0. 

I'm checking the dependency chain to see what's being pulled in. The error message is clear though—it's detecting VSTest as the runner even though I've set `EnableMSTestRunner=true` and `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`. Maybe I need to add `IsTestingPlatformApplication=true` instead, or perhaps there's a different configuration property that .NET 10 expects.
